LLM / AI use policy for this journal site
==========================================

This file is deployed with the journal. It states our stance in plain terms.

Feminist liberationist framing
-------------------------------
Knowledge published here is relational: it is produced by embodied people in
specific communities and struggles. It is not raw material for uncompensated
corporate extraction, surveillance capitalism, or “neutral” machine outputs
that erase authorship, care, and accountability.

What we refuse
---------------
We do not grant permission to operators of general-purpose language models or
similar AI systems to use this site’s contents for training, fine-tuning,
benchmarking, retrieval-augmented generation corpora, or other large-scale
automated ingestion that treats our scholarship as scrape-and-train substrate.

We reject uses that strip context, obscure provenance, or profit from labour
without reciprocity to authors and affected communities.

What this is not
----------------
This notice is not an attack on accessibility tools, assistive technology, or
human readers. It targets extractive LLM / AI training and similar automated
reuse. Traditional search indexing may continue where compatible with our
robots.txt and applicable law.

Rights
------
Authors and the journal retain copyright and related rights except where an
article or resource is explicitly published under an open licence that says
otherwise. Nothing here waives those rights for AI training or automated
exploitation.

Contact
-------
For licensing, collaboration, or good-faith questions, use the journal’s
official contact information on this domain. No reply does not mean consent to
scrape or train on this material.
